name: Grilled Chicken Salad - Daley
recipeIngredient: 8 ounces Chicken breast halves, without skin
recipeInstructions:
1. Whisk together thoroughly the lemon juice, soy sauce, garlic, black pepper, and basil in a large bowl and set aside.
2. Place the chicken on a sheet of plastic wrap spread on a work surface and cover it with a second sheet. Pound gently with a mallet. Lift the top sheet of plastic, flip the pieces of chicken over and re-cover. Evenly pound the second side to a thickness of 1/4 inch. Transfer the chicken to the marinade bowl and cover. Marinate in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
3. Preheat the grill or broiler.
4. Place the onion rounds in a single layer on a baking sheet and spray lightly with vegetable oil, turning them over and spraying to coat the other side. Remove the chicken from the marinade and place alongside the onion rings. Grill or broil the onion rounds and the chicken for 5 minutes per side. Let the chicken cool slightly, then slice thinly.
5. Spread an even amount of mixed greens on 4 salad plates. Scatter the onion rounds and then the sliced chicken on top. Sprinkle blue cheese over each. Garnish with tomato wedges.
6. Combine the dressing ingredients (lemon juice, vinegar, garlic, and basil) in a blender and mix at low speed until the garlic is finely chopped. Spoon the dressing onto the salads or serve it on the side.
